meaning_preservation.justification: The instruction and target sentence are both rendered with their core content intact; 'no one understands me but you' becomes 'no person get me but you' which is recoverable but loses the nuance of 'understand'.

fluency.justification: Readable but strained: 'group of words that can be true or not true or not' is awkward and the double 'or not' is confusing.

no_fabrication.justification: No invented content; the rewrite mirrors the source.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: 'Statement' as 'group of words that can be true or not true' is a reasonable paraphrase; 'understand' rendered as 'get' is informal but acceptable.

notes: Instruction-clause paraphrase is a bit clunky.

meaning_preservation.justification: The instruction clause is dropped entirely; only the target sentence is translated, and the size comparison is preserved but the framing task is lost.

fluency.justification: The descriptions are long but readable; the sentence parses.

no_fabrication.justification: No invented entities, but the prefix 'the words:' is a small scaffolding artifact not in the source.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Alligator and crocodile are distinguished only by 'fresh water' vs 'water that is not fresh' — a real but lossy distinction; both get the same root descriptor which collapses much.

notes: D4 here is exactly the alligator/crocodile test case from the rubric.

meaning_preservation.justification: Core question is preserved but 'fresh water' qualifier is dropped from 'alligator' compared to logic-01-02, introducing internal inconsistency; reptile and mammal distinctions are conveyed.

fluency.justification: Readable but the embedded descriptions stack heavily and the sentence becomes very long.

no_fabrication.justification: No invented content beyond necessary paraphrase.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Reptile as 'hard skin that covers the body that is cold to the touch' and mammal as 'has hair and gives white stuff that a mother gives babies' are distinguishing and informative paraphrases.

notes: Better D4 than item 02 — captures the biological distinction.

meaning_preservation.justification: Disjunction preserved between two animal classes; alligator is again just 'lives in water' without the fresh-water qualifier from item 02.

fluency.justification: Long descriptive chains make the sentence cumbersome but parseable; 'wet stuff that comes from the body' is awkward.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Reptile as 'hard little flat things on skin' (scales) and mammal description are reasonably distinguishing; better than collapsing them.

meaning_preservation.justification: The directive 'don't let reptiles into the house' is preserved.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally despite the long reptile paraphrase.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: 'Long animals with hard flat parts on skin that are cold to the touch' is a reasonable reptile paraphrase, distinguishing from mammals.

meaning_preservation.justification: Permission to kill reptiles seen in the house is preserved.

fluency.justification: Reads fairly naturally; 'cold-blooded' uses a hyphenated compound which is borderline UGF.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: 'Cold-blooded animal with a long body' captures reptile reasonably, though slightly different paraphrase than item 05.

meaning_preservation.justification: The instruction clause is dropped; core claim is preserved but 'East Africans' renders as 'people from the east part of the big land' which is vague about which land.

fluency.justification: 'best far run people' is broken and ungrammatical; reads as a clumsy fragment.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: 'East part of the big land' could refer to anywhere; doesn't clearly name Africa. 'Distance runners' as 'far run people' is lossy.

meaning_preservation.justification: The claim that Obama is not a Democrat is preserved but heavily paraphrased; 'old leader of the big country with many people' and 'Democrat' as a help-people-policy party loses the proper-noun specificity and political nuance.

fluency.justification: Reads as natural simple English.

no_fabrication.justification: Slight editorial framing of Democrats as 'wants the group that runs the country to help people more' is a minor characterization beyond the source.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Obama as 'old leader of the big country' is fine but generic; Democrat description is a one-sided characterization.

notes: D4 on proper nouns is hit-or-miss here.

meaning_preservation.justification: Sentence is rendered cleanly: 'Some humans have wings.'

fluency.justification: Natural simple English.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Vocabulary is already simple; no paraphrase needed.

meaning_preservation.justification: Meaning fully preserved.

fluency.justification: 'Some things that have wing can not go in air' is slightly off (singular 'wing'); otherwise natural.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: 'Go in air' for 'fly' is a clean paraphrase.

meaning_preservation.justification: The question is preserved but Kenya as 'far away country' is non-distinguishing; Hawaii as 'group of land in water that are part of the country' is decent.

fluency.justification: Reads acceptably but the double 'or not or not' early on is jarring.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Kenya rendered as just 'far away country' loses identifiability; Hawaii is described better. Asymmetric handling.

meaning_preservation.justification: 'Oh no!' becomes 'No!' which is a minor distortion; grizzly bear described.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: 'Big dark bear that lives in woods' is sensible for grizzly.

meaning_preservation.justification: Directive preserved; St. Louis as 'city on the big river in the middle of the country' is a reasonable identification.

fluency.justification: Reads fairly naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: St. Louis paraphrase captures location features well.

meaning_preservation.justification: Meeting in St. Louis yesterday preserved; 'before today' for 'yesterday' is a touch vague.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: St. Louis paraphrase good; 'before today' for 'yesterday' lossy.

notes: Different paraphrase of St. Louis from item 13 — slight inconsistency.

meaning_preservation.justification: Preference not to encounter a grizzly preserved; the stray '4' from the source is dropped.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Grizzly as 'big brown animal with sharp teeth that lives in the woods' is sensible.

meaning_preservation.justification: Argument structure preserved; 'witch' rendered as 'kind of person that many say can do strange power' is a thoughtful skeptic-friendly paraphrase.

fluency.justification: Heavy repetition of the witch description in both premise and conclusion drags on the sentence.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: The witch paraphrase is informative and distinguishing — captures the folk-belief aspect.

meaning_preservation.justification: Cattle as 'big animals that give white drink' (cows) and shoes as 'foot coverings' preserve meaning; the age contrast is intact.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Cattle paraphrase is decent — milk-giving captures dairy cattle but ranch cattle are more typical. Shoes paraphrase fine.

meaning_preservation.justification: The instruction clause is dropped; the proposition is preserved but the sentence is rendered as a noun phrase (starting 'the person who is angry...') rather than a full statement, weakening propositional clarity.

fluency.justification: Awkward fragment structure; missing 'is' makes it read as a phrase rather than a sentence.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: 'Flat things we put food on' for dishes is fine. Name 'Albert' dropped, replaced with 'the person'.

meaning_preservation.justification: Sequence of washing and drying preserved; dishes given a fuller description than the simple 'dishes' but core preserved.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally despite the expanded dish description.

no_fabrication.justification: The 'and the round deep things you put food in' adds 'bowls' which wasn't in the source's word 'dishes' (which could be plates only); minor expansion.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Dishes paraphrase is reasonable, expanding to both plates and bowls.

meaning_preservation.justification: Conditional preserved; 'icy' as 'covered in hard cold water' and 'slid off the turn' as 'moved to the side and left the bend' are decent renderings.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Sensible paraphrases for icy and the slip; 'metal box' for car is workable.

meaning_preservation.justification: Instruction clause dropped; the negation of both occupations preserved; 'Albert' as 'the person we talk about' is generic.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Fireman as 'puts out fire' and fisherman as 'catches fish' are clean.

meaning_preservation.justification: The exclamation about a rhinoceros is preserved; identification clearly conveyed.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Rhinoceros as 'big animal with a sharp part on its nose' is distinctive and informative.

meaning_preservation.justification: The claim that obesity is a problem in the U.S. and has risen is preserved; 'four decades' becomes 'last four ten years' — slightly awkward but recoverable.

fluency.justification: 'Last four ten years' is clunky and reads unnaturally.

no_fabrication.justification: 'Big country of the north' adds a directional qualifier the source didn't have.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: U.S. as 'big country of the north' is vague (Canada is more 'north'); obesity paraphrase fine.

meaning_preservation.justification: The graph showing rising obesity preserved; 'Bob' becomes 'the person'; 'the rising the thing of having too much body weight' is doubly-determined and confused.

fluency.justification: 'The rising the thing of having too much body weight' contains a stray article and reads ungrammatically.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Graph as 'picture with lines' and obesity paraphrase fine.

meaning_preservation.justification: Argument that Albert isn't a fireman because dogs can't be firemen is preserved; Greyhound as 'fast running dog' is fine.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Greyhound and fireman paraphrases are sensible.

meaning_preservation.justification: The argument is preserved; 'Charlie and Violet' become 'first dog' and 'second dog', losing the proper-name distinction but preserving the propositional content.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally; sweat as 'make water from skin' is slightly clunky but okay.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Sweat as 'make water from skin' is sensible; the dog-name substitution is fine.

meaning_preservation.justification: Core claim preserved but multiple details paraphrased heavily; Lynyrd Skynyrd's 'Simple Man' becomes 'the song called Simple Man by the music group that sings about a simple person' which keeps the song title but the artist becomes circular.

fluency.justification: Long winding sentence; 'making sound of words' is clunky for 'singing'.

no_fabrication.justification: 'The music group that sings about a simple person' is a slightly invented description of Lynyrd Skynyrd.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Clown, unicycle, song-singing are all paraphrased reasonably; the band-as-'group that sings about a simple person' is circular and lossy.

meaning_preservation.justification: The claim that Bob's stated reason isn't the real one preserved; Denver as 'big city in the mountains' is reasonable.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Plane as 'flying machine' and Denver as 'big city in the mountains' are sensible paraphrases.

meaning_preservation.justification: Core claim preserved but Samsung and Apple are both paraphrased as 'big maker of phones' and the distinction is weakly maintained via 'also makes small computers you can hold in a hand' vs 'also makes many other things' — these are close to collapsing.

fluency.justification: The repeated long descriptors make this very hard to follow.

no_fabrication.justification: Slightly mischaracterizes Samsung (the 'also makes many other things' phrase originally seems to apply to Samsung, but it's the second-mentioned actor here that gets it — confusing); the paraphrase essentially repeats.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Samsung and Apple are essentially conflated into nearly-identical long descriptors; the distinguishing feature (Apple makes small handheld computers, Samsung makes many things) is reversed or unclear and risks meaning-collapse.

notes: This is a clear D4 failure on distinguishing proper-noun corporate entities.

meaning_preservation.justification: The argument structure is preserved; K2 rendered as 'very high mountain that is the second highest on the world' is accurate.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally; 'cold hurt on skin' for frostbite is workable.

no_fabrication.justification: No additions; stray '8' from source dropped (acceptable).

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: K2 paraphrase is informative; frostbite as 'cold hurt on skin' is sensible.

meaning_preservation.justification: The instruction clause becomes 'a kind of writing that tells why something is true:' which is the translator pre-judging the answer (claiming it's an explanation) rather than asking; this changes the task.

fluency.justification: The framing reads as if answering rather than asking; Wanda becomes 'the person' which is fine.

no_fabrication.justification: Pre-classifying the passage as 'a kind of writing that tells why something is true' is fabrication beyond the instruction.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Sentence-level paraphrases are fine.

notes: Translator embedded an answer in the instruction paraphrase.

meaning_preservation.justification: Target sentence translated cleanly, but the entire instruction clause is dropped.

fluency.justification: Reads as natural simple English.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: No difficult concepts; paraphrase is clean.

meaning_preservation.justification: Argument structure preserved; 'Bob' and 'Henry' become 'first man' and 'second man' losing names but keeping reference.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally; 'big road thing' for bus and 'two wheel thing' for bike are clunky.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: 'Big road thing' for bus is vague (could be a truck); 'two wheel thing' for bike is fine but generic.

meaning_preservation.justification: Conditional reasoning preserved; degrees rendered awkwardly as 'the hot cold number for air'.

fluency.justification: 'The hot cold number for air' repeated twice is clunky; the instruction clause is dropped.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Temperature paraphrase 'hot cold number for air' is awkward but distinguishes from other numeric measurements.

meaning_preservation.justification: Core idea preserved but 'cognitive mechanism that monitors self-talk' compressed; 'attribute to external source' rendered as 'think the inside talk comes from something outside them' is good.

fluency.justification: 'The why that some people' is ungrammatical phrasing; instruction clause as 'fight of ideas or a showing of why' is clunky.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Schizophrenia as 'mind problem that makes them hear sounds in their own head' is descriptive but treats the symptom as the definition. Self-talk and cognitive mechanism paraphrased reasonably.

meaning_preservation.justification: The argument about fracking and foreign oil is preserved despite the heavy paraphrasing; instruction clause is dropped.

fluency.justification: Run-on sentence with no punctuation; the repeated 'black stuff that makes cars go' makes it hard to follow.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Fracking as 'rock breaking to get black stuff' is sensible; foreign oil and domestic oil both use the same root descriptor with only 'from outside' as discriminator — borderline collapsing.

meaning_preservation.justification: The claim that Wanda couldn't have ridden the bus due to holiday is preserved; instruction clause dropped.

fluency.justification: Reads as natural simple English.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Holiday as 'day when many people do not work' is sensible.

meaning_preservation.justification: Argument structure preserved; Tigers and Pirates rendered as 'big cat team' and 'boat people who take things' which keeps the team-mascot references.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally though awkward.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: 'Big cat team' for Tigers is fine; 'boat people who take things' for Pirates is creative and distinguishes from Tigers but is awkward.

meaning_preservation.justification: The argument about Pompeii and Vesuvius is preserved; lava as 'hot rock that flows like water' is clear; Pompeii and Vesuvius are paraphrased.

fluency.justification: Reads naturally despite long descriptors.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Lava paraphrase is informative; Pompeii as 'old city under the big mountain' loses the historical specificity but captures the spatial relation.

meaning_preservation.justification: Causal claim about allergies and pollen in Cincinnati preserved; 'allergies' as 'nose trouble' is lossy (allergies aren't only nasal) but recoverable.

fluency.justification: Reads acceptably; 'people nose trouble' is slightly ungrammatical.

paraphrase_sensibility.justification: Cincinnati as 'big river city' is non-distinguishing (many cities are on rivers); pollen as 'tiny dust from green things' is sensible.
